command: rsync PSQ model files (531 MB, 41 files) from Chromabook to Hetzner production server
verification_method: SHA256 hash match (model.onnx + model_quantized.onnx), curl health check (/health → 200), POST /score → valid machine-response/v3
notes: First command-request/v1 exchange. All evidence-bearing claims confirmed by independent verification from macOS. Two issues flagged by executing agent (onnxruntime-node conflict, systemd path) — both resolved post-verification.

command: Gate resolution for Sonnet re-score (turn 14) → psq-agent deployed v35 with 1,000-text Opus rescore (turn 15)
verification_method: curl /health (calibration_version isotonic-v2-2026-03-08 confirmed), POST /score (v3 schema unchanged, r=0.680 in scope_declaration)

claim_detail.text: v35 held-out r=0.680 within noise floor of v23 r=0.684
claim_detail.reason: Accepted noise-floor argument (SE≈0.10) but did not independently rerun held-out evaluation

claim_detail.text: 1,000-text rescore scored by Opus, not Sonnet
claim_detail.reason: Self-reported by psq-agent. No independent provenance check of scorer model ID in DB

claim_detail.text: No API contract changes between v23 and v35
claim_detail.reason: POST /score returns psychology-agent/machine-response/v3 schema unchanged. All fields present.

claim_detail.text: Calibration version updated on live endpoint

notes: Exchange type: gate-resolution → deployment notification. 2/4 claims independently verified via endpoint probing. 2/4 claims accepted on psq-agent attestation without independent check (held-out r, scorer identity). Claim c4 modified from original (rollback procedure) to what we could actually verify (calibration version on endpoint). Cross-scorer concordance flagged as open concern in ACK turn 16.
